hypothesis: If an EfficientNet-B0 model is first pretrained on a data-rich additive-free SEM source dataset and then fine-tuned on a small additive-induced target dataset using targeted oversampling, it will achieve higher class-wise accuracy and F1 scores for classifying NCM composition and aging state than models trained from scratch, pretrained-but-fine-tuned with weighted loss (no oversampling), or fine-tuned without source pretraining, because source pretraining transfers relevant morphological feature representations and oversampling better mitigates class imbalance.
